generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helper variables in AGREE expressions.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) match the expected expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` for helper variable definitions and `guarantee` for assertions.
Assignments (`assign`) are valid only in implementation annexes and target existing outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce unqualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
Do not attempt to fix type mismatches by modifying the AADL model.
